observations.recommendation:
We recommended management the following:

Conduct a thorough analysis of the recorded accounts payable based on the recognition criteria for liabilities, otherwise cause the necessary adjustment; and

Prepare the corresponding schedule and the required disclosures and incorporate the same in the Notes to the Financial Statements (NFS) to enable the users to understand the nature of the account.

We recommended management the following:

Conduct a thorough analysis on the existence of the unreconciled balances and make the necessary adjustment, as warranted;

Prepare the required disclosures and schedules in the notes to the financial statements to enable the users to understand the nature of the account; and

Ensure that the entries made in the general journal shall reflect the true nature of events that transpired for a particular account.

We recommended Management to direct the Municipal Accountant to make a thorough analysis of the reciprocal accounts to determine the reason on the existence of unreconciled amounts and prepare the necessary entry, as warranted.

We recommended Management, in cooperation with the LDRRM Office and LDRRM Council, to optimize the utilization of LDRRMF by taking timely action in response to the current circumstance which may affect the LDRRMF Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP).

We recommended Management to direct the Municipal Accountant to come up with an action plan to strictly observe the timeline in the submission of the RCI, together with paid DVs, RCD, together with duplicate copies of accountable forms, ROD together with paid payrolls, and Liquidation Reports to the auditor concerned.

We recommended Management to direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to assess the existing procedures in the preparation of contracts and purchase orders as well as the submission of the same to the auditor concerned and identifying the gaps and make the necessary corrective actions to comply with the requirement of COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended management the following:

Formulation of policy relative to the acceptance of delivered items taking into consideration the manpower requirement and specific procedures to be followed which will serve as a unified guidelines for compliance within the LGU;

Thereafter, direct the designated supply and property custodian to notify the auditor concerned of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries within 24 hours from acceptance thereof; and

Direct the inspection officer, to prepare and furnish the auditor concerned with a copy of the inspection report within 24 hours from acceptance of the items delivered

We recommended management the following:

Enforcement of the withholding of salary for failure to liquidate in accordance with the applicable provision of COA Circular No. 97-002; and

Formulation of policy adopting the guidelines on the grant and liquidation of cash advances set forth in Section 89 of PD No. 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002, EO No. 77 and other applicable laws, rules, and regulations.

Recommendation
We recommended that Management ensure that Municipal Accountant:
Reclassify the CIP - Building and Other Structures account totaling ₱9.995 million to the appropriate PPE account;
Recognize the depreciation expense of the completed project from the time it was completed and accepted by the agency; and
Conduct detailed review of accounting records, journal entries and supporting documents related to the CIP from previous years and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to ensure the accuracy financial statement reporting.
Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to ensure that:
Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prepare the corresponding ICS for all semi-expendable properties issued to end-users and maintain the SPC for each class of semi-expendable property.
Municipal Accounting Office to:
Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify all tangible items costing below ₱50,000.00 that were recorded as PPE as of December 2024, consistent with COA Circular No. 2022-004;
Prepare and maintain the corresponding SPLC; and
Ensure full implementation of the change in accounting policy for the procurement of equipment below the ₱50,000.00 threshold moving forward.

Recommendation
We reiterated our recommendations that Management ensure that the Municipal Accountant:
Trace, review and prepare analysis of the accounts payable for the past years based on the remaining available documents; and
Effect the necessary correcting entries and update the subsidiary ledgers/schedules so as to fairly present the account balance in the financial statements.

Recommendation
We reiterated our recommendation, and Management agreed to enforce the withholding of salaries of officers and employees who fail to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period, in accordance with the applicable provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.
We also recommended, and Management agreed to ensure that Municipal Accountant to trace, review and reconcile the unliquidated cash advances from previous years using the available books of original entry to substantiate the unidentified balances.
Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to:
Secure and consolidate supporting documentation of the dormant receivables deemed uncollectible and initiate the process of requesting for authority for their write-off, pursuant with the guidelines prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-005 to clear the accounting records and to reflect the true status of the receivables as reported in the Financial Statement; and
Provide for an appropriate impairment allowance or loss on dormant receivables to prevent overstatement of assets and ensure compliance with Paragraph 74 of IPSAS 41 on impairment of financial assets.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to ensure that the Municipal Accountant:
Prepare corresponding reciprocal entries for every transaction recorded under the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds to ensure reconciled balances at all times; and
Conduct an analysis and reconciliation of prior years’ balances for the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and effect the necessary adjusting journal entries in the books of accounts.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to ensure that the:
Municipal Accountant to:
Together with the various offices inventory custodian and Inventory Committee, to conduct physical inventory count of supplies and materials to establish the inventory balance on hand in the books of accounts;
Record all procured supplies and materials are initially recorded as inventory and recognized as expenses only upon actual issuances; and
Investigate the ₱95,933.00 non-moving inventory balance from prior years and prepare adjustments and/or write-offs, if necessary.
RHU and MEI to:
Assign a designated inventory custodian responsible for tracking supplies and maintaining updated records/reports; and
Implement inventory monitoring report and submit SSMI to Accounting as basis for recognition of supplies expenses.
Inventory Committee to:
Conduct semestral physical inventory of supplies inventories and submit the RPCI to the Officer of the Auditor.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to ensure that the:
Municipal Agriculture Office to:
Provide the Municipal Accounting Office the necessary documents to support the derecognition of dead cattle; and
Prepare and maintain WOABSPC to accurately track additions and dispositions of the breeding stocks.
Municipal Accounting Office to:
Prepare and maintain the WOABSLC to record details on the acquisitions, dispositions and changes in the fair market value of breeding stocks; and
Effect the necessary entries to derecognize dead cattle and adjustments on the fair market value of the breeding stocks to ensure fair presentation of the Biological Assets account in the Statement of Financial Position.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to ensure the following:
Municipal Accountant record expenses according to the nature of the transactions involved to prevent accounts misstatement and ensure the fair presentation of the financial statements; and
Finance Review Committee exercise due diligence in the identification of appropriate accounts for PPAs during the preparation of LEP.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to align the preparation of the NFS with the disclosure requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-004 and of the pertinent IPSAS provisions to provide additional information to the users and readers in analyzing the statements and understanding of the contents included in the NFS.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to facilitate the conduct of the one-time cleansing of PPE by undertaking the following:
Capacitate the Inventory Committee, through attendance to orientation or training on the “One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances,” to enhance their understanding of the requirements and procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006.
Inventory Committee to:
Prepare and submit the approved PIP to the COA Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of the inventory-taking process;
Complete the physical inventory of PPE in accordance with Item 6.2 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
Prepare and submit the RPCPPE covering all the PPE accounts to the Property Section and Municipal Accounting Office for reconciliation.
Property Section and Municipal Accounting Office to undertake collaborative procedures to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records and reconciled. The reconciliation shall be completed within 10 days from rendition of the RPCPPE by the Inventory Committee.

Recommendation
We recommended that Management:
Discontinue the practice of granting cash advances to unbonded officers and employees to ensure compliance with existing regulations;
Require full liquidation of all unliquidated cash advances to SDOs within the time frame prescribed and/or at year-end as provided in COA Circular No. 97-002; and
Ensure all Accountable Officers are properly bonded in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002 and Treasury Circular No. 02-2019 before approving cash advances to safeguard public funds.

Recommendation
We recommended that Management ensure that the Office of the Municipal Accountant:
a. Include in the monthly submissions of financial transaction documents the RODs for liquidation and RADAI for paid payrolls along with the payroll register/prooflist; and
b. Strictly adhere to the prescribed timeline in the submission of the DVs, ORs, payrolls and liquidation reports, along with their corresponding reports - RCIs and RCDs in accordance with 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Section 5 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

Recommendation
We recommended Management to ensure that:
All contractors install signboards in accordance with Section 2 of COA Circular 2013-004, in compliance with the respective program of works, and monitor compliance thereto; and
Signboards contain complete project details, including contact information for government agencies handling concerns or complaints.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to:
Ensure the full implementation of PPAs charged under LDRRMF by anticipating and addressing challenges such as failed bidding processes and procurement delays;
Conduct regular monitoring and evaluation of LDRRMF funded projects to track progress and take immediate action on unimplemented PPAs to prevent fund underutilization; and
Henceforth, ensure that PPAs identified during the preparation of the annual LDRRMIP are implementable and, if possible, fully executed within the budget year to enhance disaster preparedness efforts.
Recommendation:
We recommended, and Management agreed, through the LDRRMO, to ensure the use of the format for the LDRRMFIP and Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF as prescribed in COA Circular 2012-002.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed, though the GFPS, strict adherence to the timelines in the submission of GAD AR, in accordance with the provisions in COA Circular No. 2014-001 and PCW-DILG-NEDA-DBM JMC No. 2016-01.

We recommended management the following:

Direct the Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit an assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties to the Municipal Treasurer on or before the 31st day of December each year, starting calendar year (CY) 2024;

Direct the Municipal Accountant to make a thorough analysis on RPT Receivable and SET Receivable and come up with a subsidiary ledger schedule showing the details on a yearly basis; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to correct the understatement of RPT Receivable and overstatement in SET Receivable.

We recommended Management, through the Municipal Accountant, to furnish the auditor concerned of the duly receipt notices for the issuance of corresponding demand letter pursuant to Item 9.3.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended management to direct the Municipal Accountant to make a thorough review and analysis of the reciprocal accounts and determine the cause of the unreconciled amounts and prepare the necessary journal entry to adjust/correct errors, as warranted.

We recommended management the following:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the following items to its appropriate accounts (1) fund transfer from the DSWD Regional Field Office No. IX in the amounting of ₱529,255.00; (2) auditing services in the amount of ₱119,595.50; and (3) election reserved in the amount ₱18,563.62; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to incorporate the separate schedule prepared for Due to LGUs as part of the necessary disclosures in the notes to the financial statement and supply other relevant information that will enable users of the financial statement to understand composition of the said accounts such as but not limited to outstanding cash balance and the status of implementation of the related project program.

Recommendations:
Moving forward, we recommended and Management agreed to the following:
Direct the Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit an assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties to the Municipal Treasurer on or before the thirty-first (31st) day of December each year, starting CY 2024;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to make a thorough analysis on Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable and come up with a subsidiary ledger schedule showing the details on a yearly basis; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to correct the understatement of Real Property Tax Receivable and overstatement in Special Education Tax Receivable.

Recommendations:
Moving forward, we recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Conduct a thorough analysis on the recorded accounts payable based on the recognition criteria for liabilities, otherwise cause the necessary adjustment; and
Prepare the corresponding schedule and the required disclosures and incorporate the same in the NFS to enable the users to understand the nature of the account.

Recommendations:
Moving forward, we recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Conduct a thorough analysis on the existence of the unreconciled balances and make the necessary adjustment, as warranted;
Prepare the required disclosures and schedules in the notes to the financial statements to enable the users to understand the nature of the account; and
Ensure that the entries made in the general journal shall reflect the true nature of events that transpired for a particular account.

Recommendation:
Moving forward, we recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to make a thorough analysis of the reciprocal accounts to determine the reason on the existence of unreconciled amounts and prepare the necessary entry, as warranted.

Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed through the Municipal Accountant to furnish the auditor concerned of the duly receipt notices for the issuance of corresponding demand letter pursuant to Item 9.3.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

Recommendation:
Moving forward, we recommended and Management agreed in cooperation with the LDRRM Office and LDRRM Council to optimize the utilization of LDRRMF by taking timely action in response of the current circumstance which may affect the LDRRMF Investment Plan.

Recommendation:
Moving forward, we recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to come up with an action plan to strictly observe the timeline in the submission of the RCI, together with paid DVs, RCD, together with duplicate copies of AFs, ROD together with paid payrolls, and Liquidation Reports to the auditor concerned.

Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the BAC to assess the existing procedures in the preparation of contracts and POs as well as the submission of the same to the auditor concerned and identifying the gaps and make the necessary corrective actions to comply with the requirement of COA Circular No. 2009-001.

Recommendations:
Moving forward, we recommended and Management agreed to the following:
Formulation of policy relative to the acceptance of delivered items taking into consideration the delivery system to be adopted, manpower requirement and specific procedures to be followed which will serve as a unified guidelines for compliance within the LGU;
Thereafter, direct the designated supply and property custodian to notify the auditor concerned of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance thereof; and
Direct the inspection officer, to prepare and furnish the auditor concerned with the copy of inspection report within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of the items delivered.

We recommended management the following:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough analysis on the existence of the unidentified balances as of December 31, 2019 and make the necessary adjustment / correction or disclosures, if warranted;

Direct the Municipal Accountant to incorporate the separate schedule prepared in the noted to the financial statements and provide other relevant information needed; and

Moving forward, the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the entries made in general journal shall reflect the true nature of events that transpired for a particular account.

We recommended management to direct the Municipal Accountant to incorporate the separate schedule prepared and provide other necessary disclosure that will enable users of the financial statement to understand the existence of such account such as but not limited to nature and purpose of the trust receipts and the status of its corresponding cash balance.
We recommended management to direct the Municipal Accountant to Prepare the corresponding adjustment to correct the erroneous entry pertaining to the outright recording of expenses on purchases made.

We recommended management the following:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the timeline for the submission of reports and financial transaction documents to the Accounting Office to facilitate the timely recording of the same;

Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the reports and financial transaction documents covering the affected period/months immediately; and

The Municipal Accountant to make the necessary action plan to address the delay in the submission of the same in the subsequent period.

We recommended management the following:

Establishment of hiring policies/guidelines and criteria (designation, function, qualification, specific/ lump sum/intermittent work/job and services, etc.);

Evaluate the necessity of 72-hour work shift for rescuers and establish work shifting of 24-hour work shift instead for continuous service delivery on a daily basis; and

Evaluate the staffing pattern of the municipality to fill up the vacant and funded positions of the LGU in order to limit the hiring of contract orders and job order employees and be guided by the pertinent provisions of the Civil Service Commission.

The Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of reciprocal accounts. so that only the net amount shall be reflected in the financial statements by undertaking a detailed review of the accounts and determine the cause of the unreconciled amounts; and 

Thereafter, to make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts.
The Municipal Accountant to communicate with the parties involved and prepare the necessary journal entries.
The Municipal Accountant to trace the transactions relating to the unreconciled items involved and prepare the necessary journal entries.

Direct the completion of the conduct of the annual physical count of PPEs and Inventories and come up with the RPCPPE and RPCI and accordingly prepare the PIF to be submitted to the Audit Team and GSIS to secure insurance.

The Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Local Road Network Property Card,	Local Road Network Ledger Card, Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network and the Report on Local Road Network; and

The Municipal Accountant to maintain the subsidiary ledger for roads and its components and reconcile the same to the result of the physical count at year end and to properly depreciate the account; and to properly disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

The Municipal Assessor to update the Assessment Rolls as to the conditions of the real properties, its actual use, and the exemptions on RPT, if any, and to regularly conduct real property assessment every three (3) years in accordance with the existing laws, rules and regulations.
Preparation of a 10-year SWMP taking into consideration the various recommendations made by the NSWMC.

The Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer to regularly update and maintain Property Cards, issue the necessary PAR for those issued PPEs under the accountability of the end users and to renew the same every three (3) years or as soon as there is a change in custody as required in the PSM Manual to strengthen control and accountability over government assets; and

Moving forward, with the adoption of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs to strictly follow the provisions for Accounting and Property Records to be maintained for PPE as described in Section 269 of Volume I.
Establishment of GAD monitoring and evaluation system as required under Sections 3.4 and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended that management immediately correct the noted deficiencies to ensure the safety of the occupants of the Buildings owned and so that no fines, penalties, abatements or closures are imposed on the agency.
The Local Chief Executive improve the internal control system of the LGU by addressing the gaps presented.

We recommended management to direct the Municipal Accountant:
to conduct a thorough analysis on the discrepancy between the ledger balance and the balance presented in the financial statements in the General Fund and Trust Fund accounts; and
to record the reconciling items identified during the preparation of bank reconciliation statement in a timely manner to ensure that cash in bank will be brought to its adjusted balance.

We recommended management the following:
direct all accountable officers and employee to settle immediately the outstanding cash advance(s) which have been due for liquidation; and
formulate policy adopting the guidelines on the grant and liquidation of cash advances set forth on COA Circular 97-002 and Section 89 of P.D. 1445.

We recommended management to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
make a thorough review and analysis of the reciprocal accounts and determine the cause of the unreconciled amounts; and
prepare the necessary journal voucher to adjustment/correction the unreconciled amounts in the books of accounts.

We recommended management the following:
come up with an action plan in accordance with the guidelines and specific procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006 availing the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts;
thereafter, direct the municipal accountant and municipal treasurer to prepare and maintain the property, plant and equipment ledger card (PPELC) and property card (PC), respectively; and
designation of property custodian.

We recommended management the following:
make representation with the DPWH - 1st District Engineering Office to secure copy of the journal entry voucher and supporting documents used as basis in the dropping of assets in their books of account as reference; and
thereafter, direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entry voucher to recognized the cost of the hospital as assets and to set-up the related accumulated depreciation.

We recommended management to direct the Municipal Agriculturist to produce the document/s evidencing the distribution/dispersal of livestock to various beneficiaries and furnish the Office of the Municipal Accountant and direct the latter to prepare the necessary entry to derecognized the Biological Assets in the books of accounts of the LGU based on the documents presented by the former.

We recommended management the following:
direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entry to reclassify the affected accounts into its appropriate accounts; and
direct the Municipal Accountant to restate the financial statements for CY 2021 for comparative presentation.

We recommended management the following:
direct the Municipal Treasurer to make the necessary correction in the cash book by recording all disbursement made in CY 2021 whether through issuance of check or Advice to Debit account (ADA); and
moving forward, direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to carry out the necessary reconciliation of records at least quarterly.

We recommended management the following:
make representation with the Office of the Provincial Assessor to undertake general revision of assessments and property classification which has been long overdue; and
make representation with the Office of the Sangguniang Panlalawigan through the Secretary to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan to pass a resolution relative to the general revision of assessments and property classification.

We recommended management the following:
designation of personnel who will accomplish the Property Inventory Form (PIF) upon completion of the RPCPPE and RPCI by the Inventory Committee; and
submission of the accomplished PIF to Government Insurance Fund (GIF) of the GSIS as basis for the assessment of general insurance coverage over all insurable assets and properties of the LGU and apply for the insurance policy and to the auditor concerned.

We recommended management the following:
direct the Inventory Committee to include the Local Road Network in the conduct of physical count of PPEs and accomplish the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network;
thereafter, direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Report on Local Road Network as well as the maintenance of Local Road Network Property Card; and
direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Local Road Network Ledger Card and provide the required disclosures in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
We recommended that the LGU prepare it’s 10-year SWMP in accordance with the various recommendations made by the NSWMC in order for it to be approved by the NSWMC.

We recommended management the following:
strengthen the GAD Focal Point System by capacitating the GAD Focal Person thru attendance to seminar on GAD related topics, coaching, benchmarking from other LGUs and any other forms of learning; and
thereafter, direct the GAD Focal Person to establish a comprehensive GAD database that will serve as basis in planning, programming and policy formulation for gender responsive programs and activities.

We recommended management to create GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team through issuance of Executive Order or Administrative Order in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 to assess the effectiveness and efficiency of GAD mainstreaming efforts.
We recommended management to improve the internal control system by addressing the gaps presented in the previous sections.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to ascertain the amount of PPE by doing a physical count and reconciling the data with the Municipal Treasurer; and to provide depreciation to the above PPEs and to submit the lapsing schedule of depreciation for all PPEs.

The Municipal Accountant to reclassify these PPE as Investment Properties in accordance with IPSAS 16. Moving forward to the implementation of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs refer to Sections 287 to 303 of Volume I for the proper recording and reporting of Investment Properties.

The Municipal Accountant to reclassify all completed projects in the CIP account to the appropriate asset account and record the accumulated depreciation from prior years and depreciate these assets accordingly; and

Moving forward, with the issuance of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, follow the illustrated entries in Section 251 item (g), Volume I in recording the construction of PPEs.
The Municipal Accountant to properly review the COA Chart of Accounts and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations and properly classify future transactions as to their proper accounts.
The Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entry voucher (JEV) in both the General and Trust Fund to correct the erroneous entry.

The Municipal Accountant to evaluate the liabilities of the Municipality and determine the causes of the unreconciled amounts; and

The Municipal Accountant to identify these liabilities and classify them to their respective accounts, while derecognizing the non-existing liabilities by following the basic accounting principle on the recognition of liabilities and make the necessary correcting entries.

The Municipal Treasurer to determine the basic RPT and SET and to submit to the Accounting Office a certified list of taxpayers with taxes due to ensure the recording of the same at the beginning of the year and the accuracy of the related accounts; and

Moving forward, with the implementation of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, follow the prescribed entries as illustrated in Section 69, Volume I.

The Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements by revisiting the provisions of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) and other COA issuances particularly on the preparation and presentation of restated financial statements; and 

Moving forward to the implementation of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, future restatements are to be in accordance with Sections 246 and 247.

The Municipal Accountant to prepare the corresponding financial statements as prescribed in Section 110 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and Section 3.7 paragraph 1 of Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Local Budget Circular 125 and submit the same to the auditor concerned.

The Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements by revisiting the provisions of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) 2 and other COA issuances particularly on the preparation and presentation of accounts in the financial statements; and

Moving forward, with the issuance of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, to strictly follow the proforma illustrations for the Notes to the Financial Statements.
The Municipal Accountant to reconcile the difference between the actual amounts presented in the SCBAA and the amount in the Statement of Financial Performance.

The Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements by revisiting the provisions of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) particularly on the preparation and presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
The Municipal Accountant to revisit the provisions of IPSAS 42 and to properly disclose the needed information in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
The Municipal Accountant to revisit the provisions of IPSAS 14 and to properly disclose the needed information in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

The Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements by revisiting the provisions of International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) and other COA issuances particularly on the preparation and presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

Compliance with the pertinent requirements and regulations concerning the ESP and SAP by directing the MSWDO and Municipal Treasurer to timely distribute future ESPs and liquidate the same following the prescribed timeline; and

The MSWDO to come up with a wait-list and possible alternative beneficiary to serve as replacements should there be disqualified beneficiaries in order to prevent future cash returns.

Compliance with the pertinent requirements and regulations concerning the DILG-LGSF-AM Projects; and

 The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to prepare and submit all required reports.

Address the problem of lack of manpower by adding additional employees to the accounting office to augment the current workforce; and

Direct the officials and employees concerned to strictly comply with the prescribed laws, rules, and regulations on the prompt submission of the financial reports with complete documentation as basis in establishing the validity and regularity of the payments made.

Submission of list of all the on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year as well as the monthly monitoring report on the implementation of the PPAs using the prescribed form provided in the circular indicating in the remark’s column the mode of procurement and specific status of the project such as on-going, for implementation, completed, suspended/ cancelled, terminated and for bidding.

The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to coordinate with each other in order to prevent future delays in computations and preparations of pertinent accounting documents to facilitate the prompt release and to release immediately any unreleased shares to the barangays concerned.
Prioritize the processing of the titling of the lands to prove claim of ownership and to safeguard government interest.

Provision of safety equipment to the garbage collectors;

The Head of the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office to enforce and monitor compliance on the wearing of the complete, basic safety gears and equipment by all personnel who are directly dealing with the collections of solid wastes of the Municipality.
Strict adherence to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for the timely submission of the said reports to COA Audit Team.
The Human Resource Management Officer to revise the leave balances of the affected Municipal Employees.

Compliance with the pertinent requirements and regulations concerning the transparency and accountability in the allocation and utilization of the SEF as required by DepEd, DBM, and DILG JC No. 01; and

The Municipal Accountant to timely prepare and submit all the required reports.

Require the Local Economic Enterprise Officer to prepare the 5-Year Staffing Plan and Staff Transition Strategy and Five (5) Year Business Plan for each Local Economic Enterprise. Also, prepare the semi-annual SAFSSR needed to support the FFSE.
Strict adherence with the requirements and provisions of existing laws concerning LCPC and to submit the required reports.

The Liquidating Officer shall no longer be allowed to perform as disbursing officer and that the municipality appoint/designate a new disbursing officer so that proper segregation of duties be implemented.

Review the continuing appropriations, and the current appropriations involving the use of 20% LDF and make sure that all projects will be implemented in the current year;


Consider entering into contract with competent outside contractor through public bidding, if necessary, in addition to its own capability to implement the projects.

Maximize the utilization of LDRRMF to enable the Municipality to implement projects related to disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness within the scheduled timeframe; and 

Expedite the implementation of all PPAs under LDRRM to prevent possible loss of life, damage to government property, and loss of livelihood.

The Local Finance Committee to identify additional sources of income and/or push for the passage of updated revenue code to boost local taxation and design a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue collection based on the current state of the Municipality’s economic enterprises; and 

The Municipal Treasurer to monitor, intensify and strategize collection efforts from Service and Business sources and taxpayers such as deployment of demand letters and tapping the assistance of the barangays for real property collections.

The Municipal Accountant analyze and reconcile the contribution premiums withheld for the GSIS and remit immediately what was due to GSIS in accordance with the existing laws, rules, and regulations; and

Moving forward, remit directly to GSIS the members/employees’ contributions within the first ten (10) days of the calendar month following the month to which the contributions apply.
The Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit the unremitted withheld taxes to the BIR, representing the withheld VAT and income taxes from government purchases.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to prepare the necessary journal entry to recognize the transfer of asset from the DPWH in the books of accounts of the LGU.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of reciprocal accounts so that only the net amount shall be reflected in the financial statements. Further, the Accountant to make a detailed review of the accounts and determine the cause of the unreconciled amounts.  Thereafter, to make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the misstatement of liability and assets accounts. The suggested adjusting entry is presented below:
Due to NGAs					10,446,000
Due from Other Funds				10,446,000

We recommend that the management stop the practice of using the funds of the General Fund for expenses, projects and/or activities related to Trust Fund. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record all purchases of supplies and materials to the appropriate asset accounts using the perpetual inventory method regardless of whether the items will be consumed within the accounting period or not.
Also, to require the property custodian of all requisitioning office to conduct physical inventory every semester and to submit a report to the Municipal Treasurer for consolidation, so that the cost of supplies and materials actually issued/consumed may be accurately determined and taken up in the books.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant in recording procurement made by the municipality, to consider the asset recognition principle where an asset should be recognized when it is probable that future economic benefit will flow to the enterprise or agency and it can be measured reliably.
